master_row.title: Relation: S^3 xrightarrowπ S^2, π^-1(p) Cong S^1
master_row.canonical_statement: ubsection{Type I: Alfvén Resonance Ladder} \begin{theorem}[Alfvén Mode Quantization] In a spherical cavity of radius R with permeable boundary $\mu_r$, Alfvén waves satisfy: \begin{equation} \omega_{n\ell m} = \frac{v_A}{R} \sqrt{n^2 + \frac{\ell(\ell+1)}{1 + \frac{R}{2\delta_{skin}}}} \end{equation} where n, $\ell$, m are quantum numbers encoding topological winding. \end{theorem} \textbf{Achievable Today}: Frequencies 1-100 kHz in ferrofluid systems with B $\sim$ 0.1-1 T. \subsection{Type II: Hopf Fibration Harmonics} Your rotating dipole system naturally generates Hopf fibration structure: \begin{equation} S^3 \xrightarrow{\pi} S^2, \quad \pi^{-1}(p) \cong S^1 \end{equation} The fiber circles $S^1$ correspond to magnetic field lines. Acoustic standing waves along these fibers create: \begin{equation} \Psi_{Hopf}(\theta, \phi, \psi, t) = \sum_{k} c_k e^{ik\psi} e^{-i\omega_k t} Y_{\ell}^m(\theta, \phi) \end{equation} where $\psi$ parameterizes the S$^1$ fiber (the "hidden" dimension). \subsection{4D Sound in 3D Space} By modulating acoustic frequency to sweep through k-modes, you can \textbf{effectively "play" the fourth dimension} like a musical instrument
